sanskrit: गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत्। प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम्॥
transliteration: gatir bhartā prabhuḥ sākṣī nivāsaḥ śaraṇaṃ suhṛt | prabhavaḥ pralayaḥ sthānaṃ nidhānaṃ bījam avyayam ||
literal_meaning: I am the goal, the supporter, the Lord, the witness, the abode, the refuge, the friend; the origin, the dissolution, the ground, the resting place, the imperishable seed.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists twelve existential roles he occupies in the psyche, offering a single relational anchor that replaces fragmented object-relations with one internalized secure base.
psychological_state: Secure existential attachment—shifting from many partial self-objects to one integrated Self-object that holds every developmental need.
trigger_scenario: A client who has cycled through therapists, self-help gurus, and spiritual communities suddenly senses one internal presence that has never left, never misattuned, and never needs to be replaced.
anchor_text: Rest in the one inner presence that holds, witnesses and outlives every changing role.
